title: trisetum spicatum var. molle
intro: Trisetum spicatum var. molle, also known as soft speargrass, is a variety of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae and is native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce pain. The health benefits of Trisetum spicatum var. molle include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Trisetum spicatum var. molle can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications to promote skin health and reduce inflammation.
